source: South Sudan Ministry of Health Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI); WHO/UNICEF JRF 2024 country profile; WHO AFRO South Sudan; UNICEF South Sudan country pages; Gavi South Sudan
source_notes: Schedule composed from WHO/UNICEF JRF South Sudan 2024 country profile, WHO AFRO South Sudan, Gavi South Sudan, and the WHO Immunization Data portal for South Sudan (SSD). WHO-aligned 6/10/14-week primary series. South Sudan introduced PCV13 (2014), Rotavirus (2014), and IPV at 14 weeks. HepB birth dose is NOT routine in public EPI — HepB is delivered via Pentavalent only, similar to Ethiopia. Yellow Fever risk is present in parts of South Sudan; YF given in YF-endemic states. MenA in meningitis-belt areas. Coverage substantially affected by ongoing displacement and humanitarian conditions.
verification_notes: Phase 1 seed marked needs_review on the country selector. Do NOT treat as clinically authoritative until row-level reconciliation against the current WHO/XMart 2024 JRF schedule and South Sudan MoH EPI bulletin is complete. Open items include: HepB birth dose introduction status, current PCV product, MR (Measles-Rubella) integration timing, MenA / YF subnational targeting, coverage gaps in displacement-affected areas.
source_year_or_release_date: 2024 WHO/UNICEF JRF schedule data; seed first added 2026-04-25
scope: Routine childhood immunisation, birth to 72 months (6 years). Compulsory (South Sudan EPI) and subnationally-routine antigens.
context_note: Source-country schedule. In cross-border reconciliation INTO Egypt, South Sudan-EPI doses are the reliable signal on a South Sudanese card (which may use English or local languages). South Sudan public EPI does NOT give a routine HepB birth dose — HepB is delivered via Pentavalent only. A South Sudanese-origin child with documented Pentavalent ×3 has completed their HepB course. Yellow Fever (subnational) and MenA doses (where given) are South-Sudan-routine but are not on the Egyptian schedule — preserve on the record but do not count as 'missing' for Egypt.
key_differences_vs_egypt: South Sudan uses WHO-aligned 6/10/14-week primary series; Egypt uses 2/4/6-month series
South Sudan uses Pentavalent (DTPw-Hib-HepB, 5-in-1, no IPV); Egypt uses Hexavalent (6-in-1, includes IPV)
South Sudan public EPI does NOT give a routine HepB birth dose — HepB is delivered via Pentavalent starting at 6 weeks; Egypt gives HepB monovalent at birth
South Sudan gives separate IPV at 14 weeks; Egypt's IPV is bundled in Hexavalent
South Sudan gives Measles monovalent at 9 months; Egypt gives MMR at 12 months and 18 months
South Sudan has routine Rotavirus (Rota1 6w, Rota2 10w) in EPI; Egypt does not
South Sudan has routine PCV13 (6/10/14w) in EPI; Egypt PCV is recommended/private only
South Sudan gives Yellow Fever at 9 months in YF-endemic states; Egypt does not (YF non-endemic)
South Sudan gives Meningococcal A (MenAfriVac) at 9 months in meningitis-belt areas; Egypt does not

doses.notes: At birth or as soon as possible after birth.
OPV0 at birth. South Sudan uses bivalent OPV (bOPV) in routine schedule.
OPV1 at 6 weeks. Co-administered with Pentavalent, PCV, Rotavirus.
Penta1 at 6 weeks. 5-in-1 covering DTPw, Hib, HepB. This is the FIRST HepB exposure in South Sudan public EPI — no routine HepB birth dose.
PCV1 at 6 weeks. South Sudan uses PCV13 (Prevenar 13).
Rota1 at 6 weeks. 2-dose oral series. Series must be initiated by 15 weeks and completed by 24 months per WHO.
Rota2 at 10 weeks. Series complete after dose 2 (Rotarix).
Penta3 at 14 weeks. Primary DTPw-Hib-HepB series complete.
IPV1 at 14 weeks. Single fIPV dose; second IPV under WHO consideration.
PCV3 at 14 weeks. Primary PCV series complete.
Measles first dose (MCV1) at 9 months. South Sudan public EPI delivers measles-monovalent at 9 months (not MMR).
Yellow Fever at 9 months in YF-endemic states. NOT given in YF-non-endemic states. WHO YF certificate is lifetime-valid.
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF South Sudan 2024; WHO YF country list

doses.notes: MenAfriVac at 9 months in meningitis-belt areas of South Sudan.
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF South Sudan 2024; WHO Meningitis Belt

open_questions: HepB birth dose introduction status — currently not routine in public EPI.
Current PCV product (PCV13 vs PCV10) — verify against most recent procurement cycle.
MR (Measles-Rubella) introduction status at MCV2 — second measles-containing dose schedule has been variable.
MenA and YF subnational targeting — exact state-level boundaries.
Coverage gaps in displacement-affected areas (Upper Nile, Unity, Jonglei).
